resolution: Answered at depth. The 12-gate bundle + two-cycle-requirement + shadow-cycle + weight-delta-cap + rollback ensemble has real rigor (numeric thresholds on 9/12 gates, 0.05 per-lane weight-delta cap, two-cycle positive-shadow rule, rollback telemetry) but three documented regression categories leak through: (A) correlated micro-perturbations slip the per-lane-independent cap because there is no L1/L2 vector-norm check, allowing 4 lanes to each drift 0.049 for a total L1 delta of 0.196 with zero cap violations; (B) oscillating skills with high within-cycle variance pass the two-cycle rule because candidateAccuracy is a scalar with no variance/bootstrap metric and two-cycle-requirement.consecutivePasses reads only the boolean passed field; (C) post-rollback cache-invalidation failure is silent — rollback.ts wraps invalidateCache in try/catch, sets cacheInvalidated=false, stashes the error in a side-channel cacheInvalidationError field, but returns rolledBack=true regardless, so callers treat rollback as complete while the in-process cache continues serving results scored under the rolled-back weights.
evidence: skill-advisor/lib/promotion/shadow-cycle.ts:140-200 (runShadowCycle; passesShadowGate defaults minimumAccuracy to liveAccuracy, maximumGoldNoneFalseFire to +Infinity; sideEffectFree hardcoded true; audit interface structural-only)
skill-advisor/lib/promotion/rollback.ts:40-72 (try/catch around invalidateCache; rolledBack always true; cacheInvalidationError side-channel)
iter 1 F35-F47 (12 gates with 9 numeric thresholds + 3 boolean)

resolution: Answered at depth. Stale-state messaging is NOT consistent across the 4 tool surfaces. Five distinct state vocabularies circulate: V1 ensure-ready.GraphFreshness {fresh,stale,empty}, V2 ops-hardening.GraphFreshness {fresh,stale,empty,error} — SAME NAME, DIFFERENT VALUE SET, structural landmine — V3 startup-brief.graphState {ready,stale,empty,missing}, V4 canonical StructuralReadiness {ready,stale,missing}, V5 SharedPayloadTrustState subset {live,stale,absent}. Four handler surfaces diverge on at least 9 axes: S1 status.ts emits trustState but no blocked/degraded; S2 context.ts emits all three (trustState+blocked+degraded) and manually injects trustState='unavailable' past buildReadinessBlock's 3-value subset on readiness-crash; S3 query.ts emits blocked+degraded but drops the trustState field entirely on readiness-throw (converts to status:'error'); S4 startup-brief.ts uses a different vocabulary (graphState V3) and is the ONLY surface that surfaces the inline-refresh user-facing behavior. Two silent degraded-state paths identified: S2's ad-hoc 'unavailable' widening beyond the canonical 3-state subset, and S3's crash-to-error transition that loses trust-state information.
evidence: code-graph/handlers/status.ts:11-63 (S1: raw freshness + canonicalReadiness + trustState; no blocked/degraded)
code-graph/handlers/query.ts:620-780 (S3: blocked+degraded+blockReason; readiness-throw drops to status:'error' with no trustState)
code-graph/lib/startup-brief.ts:223-247 (S4: inline-refresh UX message unique to startup-brief)

text: shadow-cycle.ts passesShadowGate formula at line 182-184: default minimumAccuracy=liveAccuracy (no absolute floor), default maximumGoldNoneFalseFire=+Infinity (silent disable). sideEffectFree=true is hardcoded at line 198 regardless of whether the ShadowCycleSideEffectAudit hooks were invoked — the audit surface declares intent but does not enforce the no-side-effect invariant.

Regression leakage Category A: correlated micro-perturbations. MAX_PROMOTION_WEIGHT_DELTA=0.05 is per-lane independent. A 4-lane correlated shift of 0.049 each yields L1 delta 0.196 (L2 0.098) with zero cap violations. 20 such promotions drift the vector by L2~2.0 with all shadow cycles marginally passing.

Regression leakage Category B: oscillating skills. candidateAccuracy is a scalar with no variance/bootstrap metric. two-cycle-requirement.consecutivePasses reads only entry.passed (boolean), ignoring deltaVsLive and candidateAccuracy. High within-cycle variance skills pass the two-cycle rule by chance in a noisy eval environment.

Regression leakage Category C: silent post-rollback cache staleness. rollback.ts wraps invalidateCache in try/catch; on throw sets cacheInvalidated=false and stashes error in side-channel cacheInvalidationError field; rolledBack=true regardless. Callers that read rolledBack as success treat the rollback as complete while the in-process cache keeps serving under rolled-back weights.

Two GraphFreshness type aliases exist in the code-graph package with the same name but different value sets: ensure-ready.ts:22 exports {fresh,stale,empty} while ops-hardening.ts:7 exports {fresh,stale,empty,error}. Any refactor unifying imports could produce silent widening/narrowing. Combined with startup-brief's graphState {ready,stale,empty,missing} (V3), StructuralReadiness {ready,stale,missing} (V4), and SharedPayloadTrustState {live,stale,absent} (V5), the package carries FIVE competing state vocabularies.

4-surface divergence matrix on 9 axes: S1 status.ts emits trustState but no blocked/degraded/blockReason. S2 context.ts emits all three AND manually injects trustState='unavailable' on readinessCheckCrashed (bypasses buildReadinessBlock's canonical 3-state subset). S3 query.ts emits blocked+degraded+blockReason but drops trustState entirely on readiness-throw (converts to status:'error'). S4 startup-brief.ts uses V3 graphState vocabulary and is the ONLY surface surfacing the inline-refresh UX message.
